zh.lifeSummary: Mrs Robert de Holway I於1328年之前與Robert de Holway I在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs Robert de Holway I于1328年之前与Robert de Holway I在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Holway
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Donal, Patrick James.
English (Devon and Somerset): variant of   Holloway  , perhaps specifically from Holway in Somerset.

variants.name: Holloway
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(by the) sunken road’, from Middle English hol(g)h ‘hollow’ + weie ‘way, road’ (Old English holh + weg), or else a habitational name from any of numerous places so named, such as Holloway (Middlesex) or Holway (Somerset). In Ireland (Leinster), the name has sometimes been Gaelicized as Ó hAilmhic (see Hulvey ).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Robert de Holway I married Robert de Holway I before 1328, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs Robert de Holway I a épousé Robert de Holway I avant 1328, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs Robert de Holway I hat vor 1328 in England Robert de Holway I geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs Robert de Holway I lei sposato Robert de Holway I prima del 1328, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Mrs Robert de Holway Iは1328年以前にイギリスでRobert de Holway Iと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs Robert de Holway I(은)는 1328년 이전 영국에서 Robert de Holway I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs Robert de Holway I casou-se com Robert de Holway I antes 1328, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs Robert de Holway I вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert de Holway I перед 1328 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs Robert de Holway I se casó con Robert de Holway I antes de 1328, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.

th.–Female: ถึงแก่กรรม
